notes: First-pass printable fit model for layout, service access, and 3D-print enclosure iteration.
Default print target is Bambu Lab PLA Pure on a Bambu Lab P1S with the included 0.4mm nozzle.
Verify every component with calipers before printing a functional enclosure.
Dimensions include practical clearance where noted; not all vendor pages publish exact dimensions.
The battery pocket is intentionally less tight than the hard parts; do not clamp or compress a lithium pack.

printer.notes: The 244mm enclosure, 244mm mounting plate, and 232mm removable service cover fit inside the P1S volume directly.

material.name: Bambu Lab PLA Pure
material.fit_notes: Use the Z-direction tensile value for conservative layer-adhesion checks. Avoid hot sun or sustained loads near PLA heat-deflection temperatures.

wire_routing_channels.type: symmetric open cable-comb layout with ten dedicated raised-lip lanes and snap-in retention nibs
wire_routing_channels.mounting_surface: inside rear wall of the main enclosure, accessible after removing the service cover

lanes.name: spare_high_current
lanes.status: reserved for the future high-side servo power switch

notes: The troughs are raised from the rear wall instead of cut into it, preserving the full 3.2mm structural wall thickness.
Open ends provide bend-relief zones into the battery, splitter, controller, and servo bays; do not force a wire through a sharp 90-degree corner.

The routing mirrors the clean bench map: servo positive rises on the outer left, servo ground rises on the outer right, battery feeds use the inner high-current lanes, and the five low-current controller paths remain centered.

Harness colors follow the physical build: red for positive and regulated 5V, black for shared ground distribution, yellow for PWM, and brown only for the servo ground pigtail after its three-pin connector.
Nine grooves carry the current harness. The tenth 16 AWG groove is deliberately empty so the future high-side servo power switch can be added without reprinting the enclosure.
The 1mm ribs form a printable cable comb; the outer groove edges retain about 1.7mm clearance to the 65.6mm-wide inner shell.
Wire diameters are conservative estimates for flexible silicone-insulated stranded tinned-copper wire. Measure the purchased wire and print a channel coupon before the enclosure.
Retention nibs are removable-service features, not permanent bridges. Verify snap fit and PLA fatigue on a coupon before committing to the full-height print.

printed_parts.name: door_mounting_plate
printed_parts.role: Fixed 2in x 244mm adhesive spine that stays on the door and hides behind the enclosure once the sled is installed.

printed_parts.name: main_enclosure_sled
printed_parts.role: Removable housing that slides onto the door plate and contains the servo, controller, wiring, inline lever splitters, buck, and battery slot.

printed_parts.name: removable_service_cover
printed_parts.role: Near full-height front service cover that can slide off the housing on its own low-profile dovetail rails.

enclosure.back_plate_print_note: The mounting plate is intentionally flush with the 244mm enclosure so it hides after installation. Open-ended rails plus a small hidden detent replace the earlier visible external retention geometry.

selected_strip.source: 3M product 17217 for capacity; Uline 3M 17217 dimensions table for strip footprint.

alternative_fit_summary.verdict: Selected. Tightest width fit, but highest ideal capacity on the 2in x 244mm flush plate.
Fits with more side margin, but lower ideal capacity; three rows do not fit with practical spacing.
More pieces fit, but total ideal capacity still trails four X-Large pairs.

adhesive_mount.notes: The 17217 XL strips do fit on the current 2in plate, but the side margin is only about 1.2mm. Add shallow printed placement outlines or a simple installation jig so the strips land straight.

housing_fit_simulation.notes: Simulation keeps the 72 x 34 x 244mm housing and 50.8 x 244mm flush hidden plate. Its zero-collision result covers the Phase 1.5 servo, controller/breadboard, joined inline-splitter pair, vertical buck, battery, and ten rear-wall routing grooves. The interactive Phase 1.5 viewer intentionally excludes the future external LED, solar skin, charger, and servo-switch board; those remain separate Phase 2 fit work.

model_accuracy.coordinate_system: Width is X, door-normal depth is Y in OpenSCAD and Z in the Three.js viewer, and vertical height is Z in OpenSCAD and Y in the Three.js viewer.
vendor_confirmed_geometry: XALXMAW 1-in/2-out splitter listing envelope 32 x 13 x 13.5mm
Purchased Seloky B0DM946DHG buck 60 x 40 x 10mm rounded envelope
standard_part_geometry: 170-point mini breadboard 47 x 35 x 8.5mm; shown vertically as 35 W x 8.5 D x 47 H mm
estimated_or_measure_before_print: 56mm horn thickness, hole spacing, and rubber-cap dimensions
Prototype MOSFET module component height and terminal overhang
XALXMAW splitter mass until the delivered pair is weighed
Wire bend radii, ferrules, connector float, strain relief, and hidden detent
Solar charger and final battery-monitor board envelopes
known_fit_conflicts: Two 110 x 60mm solar panels cannot occupy the same 72 x 244mm front face while preserving the current servo opening.
The prototype MOSFET module and no-solder breadboard are shown for inspection but are not included in the current zero-collision calculator result.
model_accuracy.print_gate: Measure every purchased part with calipers and replace estimated subgeometry before exporting production STL/3MF files.

slide_mount.notes: Male rails are narrowed to 30mm center spacing so both dovetails fit on a 2in-wide plate. The solid rails are 224mm long and the housing channel preview is 226mm long, leaving end margin on the flush 244mm plate. Matching female channels use the same 60 degree face angle plus 0.40mm per-side clearance so the enclosure can slide vertically while resisting straight pull-off. Print a coupon first before committing the full plate.

retention_detent.type: hidden low-profile click detent or thumb-release
retention_detent.position: inside the rail/sled interface near the upper rail span
retention_detent.notes: No bulky top or bottom stop is modeled. The sled should intentionally slide off, but a hidden detent should prevent accidental creeping during normal use.

service_cover_slide.notes: The service cover is now treated as a near full-height removable panel instead of a short hatch. It should slide into shallow cover rails or channels so maintenance access is possible without removing the enclosure from the door plate.

solar_panel_series_pair.source: 2 x 6V 1W mini solar panels, each about 110 x 60 x 3mm, wired in series for a 12V-class solar charger input
solar_panel_series_pair.notes: Surface-mounted solar skin allowance, not an internal volume block. The overall two-panel envelope fits inside the 72 x 244mm face, but the front-exposed servo pocket interrupts that face, so the final print needs a split, relief, or clip-on layout that clears the servo and service seams.

injora_35kg_servo.notes: Body pocket is tight, but the bay remains wider for tabs, fasteners, arm clearance, and wire strain relief.

servo_front_exposure_pocket.notes: Open front pocket that lets the servo protrude about 11mm from the enclosure face. Moving the body forward keeps every height detent clear of the centered controller assembly while the removable cradle still supports the servo from the back, bottom, and side walls. Final dimensions should be checked against the actual calibrated lock/unlock angles.

servo_height_adjustment_cradle.type: removable cradle on left/right notch ledges
servo_height_adjustment_cradle.notes: The servo remains tightly constrained side-to-side by the bay/cradle, while the removable floor can sit in one of three vertical detents for coarse handle alignment.

xiao_nrf52840_sense_pinned.source: Seeed Studio official XIAO nRF52840 Sense STEP model (XIAO-nRF52840 v15.step): https://files.seeedstudio.com/wiki/XIAO-BLE/seeed-studio-xiao-nrf52840-3d-model.zip
xiao_nrf52840_sense_pinned.notes: Board footprint is 21 x 17.8mm. The detailed cutaway renders Seeed's published STEP geometry, converted to an optimized color-preserving GLB, plus separate long header pins to represent the purchased pre-soldered board plugged into the vertical 170-point breadboard.

header_geometry.source: Standard 2.54mm straight male header geometry; GCT BG031 specifies 2.54mm pitch and 0.64mm square pins

mini_breadboard_170_point.source: Purchased LampVPath B01KKE602W listing: 1.85 x 1.38 x 0.33in (47 x 35 x 8.5mm)
mini_breadboard_170_point.notes: The purchased breadboard envelope is modeled exactly. Viewer orientation is vertical, so the 35mm side is enclosure width and the 47mm side is height. The XIAO is shown plugged into its front face.

xalxmaw_inline_splitter_pair.source: Purchased XALXMAW B0B28GYYL2 listing dimension graphic: 1.25 x 0.51 x 0.53in, rounded to 32 x 13 x 13.5mm
xalxmaw_inline_splitter_pair.notes: The positive and ground connectors are modeled physically clipped side-by-side at the same height, matching the clean bench map while remaining electrically separate. Their combined 64mm width leaves about 0.8mm per side inside the 65.6mm inner shell. Verify the delivered parts with calipers before printing.

detail_geometry.source: Purchased B0B28GYYL2 listing imagery and measured listing envelope; no exact manufacturer CAD model was located

servo_power_switch_module.source: Amazon MOSFET switching module listing gives 4.2 x 2.6cm board footprint; height is an enclosure allowance for terminals/components
servo_power_switch_module.layout: TBD: mount vertically in the service bay or replace with an integrated high-side switch board in the production power stack
servo_power_switch_module.notes: Prototype board is included in mass/BOM planning. Do a multimeter check before final wiring; final design should switch servo positive with a true high-side load switch or smart high-side switch.

lm2596_buck_current.source: Purchased Seloky B0DM946DHG Amazon listing: 2.36 x 1.57 x 0.39in (59.94 x 39.88 x 9.91mm), rounded to a 60 x 40 x 10mm envelope
lm2596_buck_current.notes: This is the purchased prototype buck. It is rotated vertically in the enclosure so its 40mm side spans the housing width and its 60mm side follows the clean bench stack. The detailed HTML model uses representative terminal, display, inductor, and trim-pot subgeometry. Future low-Iq regulation can shrink this bay.

battery_2s_5000mah.notes: Designed as a vertical bottom-inserting cartridge with only a small pull lip exposed. Clearance is intentionally larger than hard-part pockets so the pack is retained without compression.

future_camera_placeholders.notes: Not modeled as functional cutouts yet; reserve optional top/front service space.

mass_budget.status: engineering estimate until printed parts and real hardware are weighed
mass_basis: Vendor-published masses were used where available.
Small hardware, wire, XT30 dock, and printed PLA masses are conservative layout estimates.
Use a gram scale after the first print and update these values before trusting the adhesive calculation.

servo_horn_with_rubber_cap.source: estimate for 56mm aluminum horn plus sleeve cap

xiao_nrf52840_sense_pinned.source: estimate for XIAO board plus pre-soldered headers

xalxmaw_inline_splitter_pair.source: engineering estimate for two installed inline splitters; weigh delivered parts before final mass validation

mini_breadboard_170_point.source: engineering estimate until the actual red mini breadboard is weighed

xt30_fixed_dock_and_16awg_leads.source: estimate for fixed XT30 mating connector plus short 16 AWG leads

servo_power_switch_module.source: estimate for one MOSFET switching module with terminals; kit contains two

solar_panel_series_pair.source: estimate from 2 x 6V 1W 110 x 60mm panels; selected Amazon kit lists similar panels

solar_charge_controller_allowance.source: layout allowance for a compact 2S lithium solar charger/BMS module

battery_monitor_allowance.source: switched high-value divider prototype or tiny future fuel-gauge board

door_mounting_plate_with_dovetail_rails.source: PLA Pure density estimate from the 50.8 x 7 x 244mm plate plus rails

main_enclosure_sled.source: PLA Pure shell estimate after modeled bay cutouts

removable_service_cover.source: PLA Pure estimate for 66 x 2.2 x 232mm cover plus shallow rails

four_command_17217_xlarge_pairs.source: installation allowance for four strip pairs

quick_swap_battery_plan.preferred_connector: fixed controller-side XT30 dock that mates when the battery slides up into the bottom bay
quick_swap_battery_plan.alignment: chamfered battery slot, 0.5-1.0mm connector float, and hard stop before the pack is compressed
quick_swap_battery_plan.retention: thumb latch or spring tab plus a small pull ribbon/lip; battery should barely protrude below the enclosure
quick_swap_battery_plan.service_note: Keep 16 AWG dock leads fixed to the housing/inline-splitter path so swapping the battery does not move the internal wiring.

battery_state_of_charge_plan.prototype_lowest_idle_power: switched high-value resistor divider into the XIAO ADC, powered only during measurements
battery_state_of_charge_plan.prototype_divider_example: 1M top resistor and 330k bottom resistor maps an 8.4V full 2S pack to about 2.08V at the ADC; add a small capacitor and average readings
battery_state_of_charge_plan.future_accurate_gauge: MAX17263-class multi-cell fuel gauge for accurate SOC, time-to-empty, battery age, and charge/discharge current
battery_state_of_charge_plan.service_note: Voltage-only percentage is approximate under servo load; use filtered idle readings and low-battery thresholds before showing a polished percentage.

solar_charge_plan.selected_test_panel_kit: 3Pcs Mini Solar Panel DC 6V 1W, about 110 x 60mm per panel
solar_charge_plan.recommended_electrical_layout: use two 6V panels in series as a 12V-class input to a proper 2S lithium solar charger; never connect raw panel output directly to the 2S pack
solar_charge_plan.expected_peak_input: about 12V at 0.16A, or roughly 2W peak before charger losses when two panels are in strong sun
solar_charge_plan.mechanical_note: Treat the panels as a thin service-cover/front-face skin. The rectangular envelope fits the enclosure face, but the servo pocket interrupts that surface; final placement still needs a split, relief, or clip-on layout that keeps maintenance access.

servo_power_cut_plan.prototype_part: 2PCS 3V/5V low-voltage-control MOSFET switching module, 5-36V output, 22A listed maximum
servo_power_cut_plan.preferred_final_topology: true high-side switch on servo positive, controlled by the XIAO, with servo ground left tied to the common battery/controller ground
servo_power_cut_plan.locked_state: set PWM safe/low or high-impedance, wait briefly, then cut servo positive power
servo_power_cut_plan.unlocked_state: enable servo power before command pulses and keep power enabled only while the handle must stay held down
servo_power_cut_plan.caution: Many cheap MOSFET modules switch the low side. If the prototype module switches ground instead of positive, use it only for bench testing or add signal isolation so the servo cannot be back-powered through the PWM line.
